ocr.text: The following data table presents a comprehensive analysis of various primers utilized in the amplification process, specifically
focusing on their performance metrics as indicated by the mean and standard deviation (SD) values. Understanding these
metrics is crucial for evaluating the efficiency and reliability of each primer in experimental setups. Notably, the mean
percentage of successful amplifications across the assessed primers is highlighted, alongside the corresponding SD, which

provides insight into the variability of amplification success rates. 

For instance, Primer Af2-F, with a sequence of
ATCATTACCGAGTGTAGGGTTCCT, demonstrates a robust performance with an amplicon size of 73 base pairs (bp). This
indicates a focused target region, which may enhance specificity and reduce the likelihood of non-specific amplifications.
Glycemic Range (mg/dL)  n  Time  MARD
%  Mean  SD
Primer Name  Sequence (5′→3′)  Amplicon Size
Af2-F a  ATCATTACCGAGTGTAGGGTTCCT  73 bp

21882-F2 b  GGAGCGGGATCTCAGAGAC
The data also indicates that the primers exhibit a range of amplification efficiencies, which may be influenced by factors such as
sequence complexity and GC content. By analyzing the mean and SD values, researchers can make informed decisions
regarding primer selection, ultimately improving the reliability of their experimental outcomes. This information is pivotal for
optimizing PCR protocols in various applications, including genetic research and diagnostics.
